elements.sent-2411-001: We start with the consideration of the loop effects for light fields with non-zero mass in the expanding Poincar\'e patch of de Sitter space-time.
elements.sent-2411-002: We derive the Dyson-Schwinger equation, which sums up the leading infrared (growing with time) loop corrections in certain limit for small initial perturbations above the Bunch-Davies state.
elements.sent-2411-003: The solution of this equation shows the destiny of the initial state at the future infinity.
elements.sent-2411-004: Then we discuss the case of the contracting Poincar\'e patch and global de Sitter space-time and briefly the case of different initial conditions in the expanding Poincar\'e patch.
elements.sent-2411-005: The problem of loop corrections in different expanding space-times has gained significant attention over the past two decades \cite{Starobinsky:1994bd, Tsamis:2005hd, Marolf:2010zp, Marolf:2010nz, Higuchi:2010xt, Moreau:2018lmz, Guilleux:2015pma, Hollands:2010pr, Gorbenko:2019rza, Akhmedov:2009vs, Krotov:2010ma, Akhmedov:2017ooy, Akhmedov:2019cfd .}
elements.sent-2411-006: First, quantum fluctuations in the very early Universe are believed to have determined its present large-scale structure \cite{Starobinsky:1980te,Starobinsky:1982ee,Linde:1981mu,Linde:1983gd,Guth:1980zm,Guth:1982ec,Albrecht:1982wi .}
elements.sent-2411-007: Second, resolving the cosmological constant problem may be connected to the large infrared (IR) contributions from loop corrections, as these corrections might lead to a secular screening of the cosmological constant \cite{Polyakov:2007mm, Akhmedov:2008pu, Polyakov:2009nq, Akhmedov:2009be, Polyakov:2012uc, Akhmedov:2013vka .}
elements.sent-2411-008: Third, understanding the backreaction of quantum matter on strong gravitational fields is incomplete without accounting for loop corrections (see e.g. for the reviews \cite{Akhmedov:2013vka , \cite{Akhmedov:2021rhq ).}}
elements.sent-2411-009: Overall, it is of great interest to reduce our lack of understanding the role of quantum effects in interacting quantum field theory in various rapidly expanding backgrounds.
elements.sent-2411-010: These questions remain unresolved even in the case of de Sitter (dS) space-time, which possesses the highest degree of symmetry.
elements.sent-2411-011: The challenges arise due to the vacuum instability of dS \cite{Polyakov:2012uc , the singular behavior of the light fields \cite{Tsamis:2005hd , the breaking of global dS isometries at loop level \cite{Polyakov:2012uc , \cite{Akhmedov:2013vka .}}}}
elements.sent-2411-012: In this paper, we continue investigating these questions, following the approach of \cite{Akhmedov:2009vs,Akhmedov:2013vka,Akhmedov:2017ooy,Akhmedov:2019cfd .}
elements.sent-2411-013: We mainly focus on the case of the massive real scalar fields from so called complementary series ($m<\frac{D-1 {2}$ in units of the Hubble constant) in expanding Poincar\'e patch (EPP), except for some discussion in Section \ref{Sec:Complementary_CPP and Appendix.}}
elements.sent-2411-014: We discuss the differences in the situations in the EPP, contracting Poincar\'e patch (CPP) and in global dS.
elements.sent-2411-015: In order to set up the notations we first introduce the standard procedure of the quantization in dS and the Schwinger-Keldysh diagrammatic technique, which we will use to calculate the quantities we are interested in.
elements.subsection-2411-001: Set up of the quantization in de Sitter space-time
elements.sent-2411-016: We consider the massive real scalar self-interacting theory: \beq S[\phi] = \int d^{D x\sqrt{|g|}\left[ \frac{1 {2 g^{\mu\nu}\partial_{\mu}\phi\partial_{\nu}\phi - \frac{1 {2 m^2\phi^2 - \frac{\lambda {6}\phi^3\right], \end{equation in the expanding Poincar\'e patch (EPP) of dS space-time: \beq ds^2 = \frac{1 {\eta^2}\left(d\eta^2 - d\bm{x ^2 \right), \eeq where we take the Hubble parameter to be unit $H=1$, and $\eta = e^{-t}$ is the conformal time in the EPP.}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-017: It is ranging from $+\infty$ at the past infinity, $t\to - \infty$, to $0$ at the future one, $t\to +\infty$; $D=d+1$ is the space-time dimension.
elements.sent-2411-018: The cubic potential is taken to simplify equations below.
elements.sent-2411-019: The phenomena that we observe below have nothing to do with the instability of the cubic potential and are present also in quartic theory (see e.g. \cite{Akhmedov:2013vka , \cite{Akhmedov:2013xka ).}}
elements.sent-2411-020: The free quantum scalar field in EPP can be expanded as: \beq \begin{aligned \widehat{\phi (\eta,\bm{x )=\int\dfrac{d^{D-1}\bm{p {(2\pi)^{D-1 \bigg[ \widehat{a _{\bm{p f_{\bm{p (\eta)e^{i\bm{px + \widehat{a ^{\dagger _{\bm{p f^{* _{\bm{p (\eta)e^{-i\bm{px \bigg], \quad \left[\widehat{a _{\bm{p ,\widehat{a _{\bm{q ^\dagger \right] = (2\pi)^{D-1}\delta(\bm{p -\bm{q ), {\rm where \quad f_{\bm{p (\eta) = \eta^{\frac{D-1 {2 h_{\nu}\left(p\eta\right),\;p\equiv |\bm{p |.}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-021: To achieve the latter the modes $f_{\bm{p (\eta)e^{i\bm{px }$ should obey the Klein-Gordon equation in the inflationary metric (\ref{eq:metric ).}}}}
elements.sent-2411-023: Other options are discussed at the end of the paper.
elements.sent-2411-025: Moreover, to simplify calculations below we assume that $\nu<\frac{D-1 {6}$ and $D\leq 4$.}
elements.sent-2411-026: These restrictions will be explained below.
elements.sent-2411-027: In dS space-time one distinguishes massive fields, $m>(D-1)/2$, corresponding to the pure imaginary $\nu$.
elements.sent-2411-028: They are from the so called principal series of the representations of the dS isometry group.
elements.sent-2411-029: In dS quantum field theory there are also light fields, $m<(D-1)/2$, corresponding to real $\nu$.
elements.sent-2411-030: They are from the so called complementary series.
elements.sent-2411-031: In this paper we will be mostly considering light fields, giving only a brief comment on the principal series in the Appendix.
elements.subsection-2411-002: Schwinger-Keldysh formalism and the goal of the paper
elements.sent-2411-032: Below we perform calculations in the Schwinger-Keldysh diagrammatic technique after the Keldysh rotation (see e.g. \cite{kamenev2023field for the review): \beq \phi_{cl = \frac{\phi_{+ + \phi_{- {2 , \; \phi_{q = \phi_{+ - \phi_{- .}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-033: The state is specified by the condition $\widehat{a _{\bm{p | \text{BD}\rangle = 0$ for the above defined modes and annihilation operators.}}}
elements.sent-2411-034: In the technique in question the scalar field is characterized by the following three propagators: \beq \begin{aligned iG^K_0(\eta_1,\bm{x _1|\eta_2,\bm{x _2) = \wick{\c\phi_{cl (\eta_1,\bm{x _1)\c\phi_{cl (\eta_2,\bm{x _2) = \frac{1 {2}\bigg\langle \text{BD}\bigg| \bigg\{\widehat{\phi (\eta_1,\bm{x _1),\widehat{\phi (\eta_2,\bm{x _2) \bigg\} \bigg|\text{BD}\bigg\rangle, iG^A_0(\eta_1,\bm{x _1|\eta_2,\bm{x _2) = \wick{\c\phi_{cl (\eta_1,\bm{x _1)\c\phi_{q (\eta_2,\bm{x _2) = \theta(\eta_1-\eta_2)\rho(\eta_1,\bm{x _1|\eta_2,\bm{x _2), iG^R_0(\eta_1,\bm{x _1|t\eta_2,\bm{x _2) = \wick{\c\phi_{q (\eta_1,\bm{x _1)\c\phi_{cl (\eta_2,\bm{x _2) = -\theta(\eta_2-\eta_1)\rho(\eta_1,\bm{x _1|\eta_2,\bm{x _2), {\rm where \quad \rho(\eta_1,\bm{x _1|\eta_2,\bm{x _2) = \bigg[\widehat{\phi (\eta_1,\bm{x _1),\widehat{\phi (\eta_2,\bm{x _2) \bigg].}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-035: The Bunch-Davies state is spatially homogeneous.
elements.sent-2411-036: Hence, it is convenient to perform the spatial Fourier transformation of the propagators.
elements.sent-2411-037: After the transformation we obtain that: \beq iG^K_0(\bm{p |\eta_1,\eta_2) = \text{Re}\left\{f_{\bm{p (\eta_1)f_{\bm{p ^{* (\eta_2)\right\}, \;\; \rho(\bm{p |\eta_1,\eta_2) = -2\text{Im}\left\{f_{\bm{p (\eta_1)f_{\bm{p ^{* (\eta_2)\right\}.}}}}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-038: Meanwhile, the Keldysh propagator, $G^K_0$, is sensitive to the state of the theory.
elements.sent-2411-039: Our goal is to trace the destiny of the state of the theory at the future infinity.
elements.sent-2411-040: That is the reason why we are interested in the behavior of the loop-corrected Keldysh propagator in the limit of large external times $\eta = \sqrt{\eta_1\eta_2 \to 0$, while $\eta_1/\eta_2 =$ const, in the units of the cosmological constant.}
elements.sent-2411-041: In the momentum space (for each mode separately) this limit corresponds to $p\eta \ll |\nu|$.
elements.sent-2411-042: In this limit the loop corrected Keldysh propagator acquires the form \cite{Akhmedov:2013vka : \beq G^K(\bm{p |\eta_1,\eta_2) \simeq \left(\frac{1 {2 +n_{p (\eta)\right)f_{p (\eta_1)f_{p ^*(\eta_2) + \varkappa_{p (\eta) \, f_{p (\eta_1)f_{p (\eta_2) + \text{c.c , \eeq where in general $n_{p (\eta)$ and $\varkappa_{p (\eta)$ (perhaps multiplied by the mode functions as in (\ref{eq:Keldysh_at_large_times ) at the coincident points) are the level populations and the anomalous quantum averages, correspondingly.}}}}}}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-043: In the future infinity limit under consideration, $p\eta \to 0$, for the principal series ($m>\frac{D-1 {2}$) in the EPP the leading contributions to such products of modes as $f_{p (\eta_1)f_{p ^*(\eta_2)$ and $f_{p (\eta_1)f_{p (\eta_2)$ are different functions of $\eta$'s, as follows from (\ref{eq:asymptotics ).}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-044: Then, in such a case one can separate IR loop contributions to $n_{p (\eta)$ and $\varkappa_{p (\eta)$ from each other.}}
elements.sent-2411-045: However, for the complementary series in the EPP the leading contributions in the IR limit are the same, $f_{p (\eta_1)f_{p ^*(\eta_2) \simeq -f_{p (\eta_1)f_{p (\eta_2)$.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-046: As a result, one obtains that \beq G^K(\bm{p |\eta_1,\eta_2) \simeq \frac{A_-^2 \eta^{D-1 {(p\eta)^{2\nu N_{p (\eta), \eeq where $N_{p (\eta) = 1+2n_{p (\eta)-2\text{Re}\left\{ \varkappa_{p (\eta)\right\}$ and we have used asymptotic form of the modes (\ref{eq:asymptotics ).}}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-047: Moreover, in this case the leading first loop contributions to $n_{p (\eta)$ and $\varkappa_{p (\eta)$ cancel each other within $N_p(\eta)$, as is shown in \cite{Akhmedov:2017ooy .}}}
elements.sent-2411-048: We will show that below again.
elements.sent-2411-049: As demonstrated in several works \cite{Krotov:2010ma,Akhmedov:2009vs, Akhmedov:2013vka,Akhmedov:2013xka,Akhmedov:2017ooy,Akhmedov:2019cfd , in the future infinity limit, $p\eta \ll |\nu|$, of the EPP the Keldysh propagator at one-loop order (in $\phi^3$ theory) receives secularly growing contributions of the form $\sim \lambda^2\log(p\eta/|\nu|)$.}
elements.sent-2411-050: They become comparable in magnitude to the tree-level contribution (\ref{eq:propagators_list ) for a long enough time of evolution of the system.}
elements.sent-2411-051: This is true both for the heavy fields from the principal series and for the light ones from the complementary series (see, however, the discussion in the Appendix below).
elements.sent-2411-052: At the same time, one loop corrections to the retarded and advanced propagators are not growing as $p\eta \to 0$ and are just suppressed by $\lambda^2$.
elements.sent-2411-053: These large IR secular contributions drastically correct the physical effects seen at the tree-level, making it necessary to resum at least the leading multi-loop corrections of the form $\sim \left(\lambda^2\log(p\eta/|\nu|)\right)^n$ (assuming that $\lambda^2$ is small, while $\log(p\eta/|\nu|)$ is large).
elements.sent-2411-054: In this paper, we build upon and refine previous works on loop calculations for light fields in dS in the limit that was described above.
elements.sent-2411-055: Specifically, in the Sections \ref{subSec:One_loop_Complementary_EPP , \ref{subSec:Multy-loop and \ref{subSec:Vertices we compute the leading secularly growing contributions at one loop level and demonstrate that multi-loop ``bubble inside bubble'' diagrams, as well as corrections to multi-point correlation functions, are suppressed.}}}
elements.sent-2411-056: Based on these observations in the Section \ref{subSec:Resummation_complementary_EPP we write down the Dyson-Schwinger equation, which resums the leading corrections from all loops in the limit under consideration in the EPP.}
elements.sent-2411-057: These observations have already been made in \cite{Akhmedov:2019cfd , but no correct resummation of the leading contributions was provided.}
elements.sent-2411-058: In Section \ref{Sec:Complementary_CPP we perform similar calculations for the light fields in the contracting Poincar\'e patch (CPP).}
elements.sent-2411-059: The latter are having such a form as $\lambda^2\log\left(\frac{\eta {\eta_0}\right)$.}
elements.sent-2411-060: Here $\eta_0$ is the position of the initial Cauchy surface in the CPP.
elements.sent-2411-061: The case of CPP is discussed here for three reasons.
elements.sent-2411-062: First, the consideration of CPP is quite an important question even from the observational point of view and not only from the academic perspective.
elements.sent-2411-063: In fact, the same type of secular divergence as we just mentioned is present in the global dS space-time.
elements.sent-2411-064: That can be understood e.g. from the observation that the global dS is the union of EPP and CPP.
elements.sent-2411-065: At the same time we do not really know what was the initial state of our Universe at the start of the inflation.
elements.sent-2411-066: We do not know neither the initial Fock space state nor the geometry of the initial Cauchy surface.
elements.sent-2411-067: The state definitely was not the Bunch-Davies one at the light-like initial surface of the EPP.
elements.sent-2411-068: Otherwise even galaxies will not exist because the situation would be absolutely symmetric.
elements.sent-2411-069: The natural question is, once the start of the inflation was from a non symmetric state, what was achieved the first -- the equilibration or the strong backreaction on the background dS geometry \cite{Akhmedov:2021rhq ? Second, in CPP we encounter a long-memory effect in dS \cite{Krotov:2010ma (see also \cite{Akhmedov:2012dn,Akhmedov:2013vka ), manifesting itself as the secular divergence.}}}
elements.sent-2411-070: To cut this divergence one needs to introduce into the loop corrections the dependence on the position of the initial Cauchy surface, $\eta_0$, thus, breaking the symmetry of the tree-level theory under the dS isometry group.
elements.sent-2411-071: This phenomenon was previously treated in works \cite{Akhmedov:2012dn,Akhmedov:2013vka for the principal series in CPP and global dS within the kinetic approximation.}
elements.sent-2411-072: There it was shown that whether the symmetry under the dS isometry is restored in the exact correlation functions or not depends on the initial conditions.
elements.sent-2411-073: Third reason to consider the situation in CPP is as follows.
elements.sent-2411-074: Unlike the case of the resummation in the EPP, the corresponding resumming equations in CPP are non-linear.
elements.sent-2411-075: The reason for that is as follows: due to the secular divergence different type of diagrams is providing the leading corrections.
elements.sent-2411-076: This fact making the resummation for light fields in CPP and global dS more challenging, particularly because the kinetic approximation is not applicable in this context.
elements.sent-2411-077: We will address this issue in future work.
elements.sent-2411-078: Additionally, in Appendix \ref{App:Principal_EPP , we revisit the resummation of leading logarithms in EPP for the principal series.}
elements.sent-2411-079:
We argue that for heavy fields, loop corrections exhibit an additional suppression of the form $e^{-\pi
u}$, on top of the $\lambda^2$, thus, behaving as $\sim e^{-\pi
u}\lambda^2\log\left(p\eta/|
u|ight)$.
elements.sent-2411-080: Finally, we show that for sufficiently large masses, $\nu \gtrsim \frac{2 {\pi}\log\left(\frac{1 {\lambda}\right)$, the level populations and the anomalous quantum averages attain small values at late times, resulting in only a slight perturbation to the initial Bunch-Davies state.}}
elements.sent-2411-081: In this section we single out the leading contribution from the one-loop correction to the Keldysh propagator in the limit $p\sqrt{\eta_1\eta_2 \equiv p\eta \to 0$ for the Bunch-Davies initial state.}
elements.sent-2411-082: Having the tree-level vertices from (\ref{eq:Interaction_term ) and the propagators from (\ref{eq:propagators_list ) we can write all the relevant one-loop diagrams, which are shown on the Fig.\ref{Fig:BubbleDiagram .}}}
elements.sent-2411-083: Keeping in mind that we are interested in secular effects in the limit, when $(t_1 + t_2)/2 \gg |t_1 - t_2|$, where $\eta_{1,2 = e^{-t_{1,2 }$, in the arguments of the corrected propagator \cite{Akhmedov:2019cfd , we can neglect the difference between the external time coordinates.}}}
elements.sent-2411-084: Then the leading one-loop correction in the momentum space is contained in the following expression: \beq \begin{aligned \Delta_{1\text{-loop G^{K (\bm{p |\eta,\eta) = \lambda^2\int\frac{d^{D-1}\bm{k {(2\pi)^{D-1 }\frac{d\eta_{3 {\eta_{3 ^{D }\frac{d\eta_{4 {\eta_{4 ^{D \bigg[-\frac{1 {2}\theta(\eta_3-\eta)\theta(\eta_4-\eta)\times \times \rho\big(\bm{p}\big|\eta,\eta_3\big)\left(G^{K^2 _{0}\big(\bm{k -\bm{p}\big|\eta_3,\eta_4\big) - \frac{1 {4}\rho^2\big(\bm{k -\bm{p}\big|\eta_3,\eta_4\big)\right)\rho\big(\bm{p}\big|\eta_4,\eta\big) + + \theta(\eta_4-\eta_3)\theta(\eta_3-\eta)\rho\big(\bm{p}\big|\eta,\eta_3\big)\rho\big(\bm{k -\bm{p}\big|\eta_3,\eta_4\big) G^K_{0}\big(\bm{k -\bm{p}\big|\eta_3,\eta_4\big)G^K_{0}\big(\bm{p}\big|\eta_4,\eta\big) + +\theta(\eta_3-\eta_4)\theta(\eta_4-\eta)G^K_{0}\big(\bm{p}\big|\eta,\eta_3\big)\rho\big(\bm{k -\bm{p}\big|\eta_3,\eta_4\big) G^K_{0}\big(\bm{k -\bm{p}\big|\eta_3,\eta_4\big)\rho\big(\bm{p}\big|\eta_4,\eta\big) \bigg] .}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-085: Furthermore, to single out the leading correction one can assume that the internal conformal times, $\eta_3, \eta_4$, are such that the IR asymptotic form (\ref{eq:asymptotics ) for the modes can be used in the external propagators \cite{Krotov:2010ma .}}
elements.sent-2411-086: It must be also noted that in doing the latter we must assume the condition that $\nu < \frac{D-1 {4}$ for the IR convergence of these expressions with $\bm{p}$ taken to zero\footnote{Actually, an accurate calculation shows that there is nothing much different happens when $\nu>\frac{D-1 {4}$, but the coefficients in the resulting expressions are slightly changed.. .}}}
elements.sent-2411-087: Second, the terms in the last two lines of (\ref{eq:1-loop_correction_general_formula_EPP ) are actually equal after the exchange of the integration variables $\eta_3\leftrightarrow \eta_4$.}
elements.sent-2411-090: To obtain the second line of (\ref{eq:dominant_contr_1-loop_EPP ) we set the upper limit of the integration over $v$ to infinity due to the rapid convergence of these integrals on much shorter intervals.}
elements.sent-2411-091: One should keep in mind that we mainly restrict ourselves to the dimensions $D\leq 4$.
elements.sent-2411-092: We see that for sufficiently large times (or low conformal times in EPP) the logarithm in (\ref{eq:dominant_contr_1-loop_EPP ) becomes large and the correction can dominate even over the tree-level Keldysh propagator, while the other contributions are suppressed as $\mathcal{O}\left(\lambda^2\right)$ in this limit.}
elements.sent-2411-093: In the same way it can be shown that other terms in (\ref{eq:1-loop_correction_general_formula_EPP ) give no additional large or growing with time contributions.}
elements.sent-2411-094: Hence, in the one-loop order the dominant correction to the Keldysh propagator (\ref{eq:1-loop_correction_general_formula_EPP ) is given by \beq \Delta_{1-\text{loop G^{K (\bm{p |\eta,\eta) \simeq G^{K _{0 (\bm{p |\eta,\eta)\cdot A\lambda^2\log\left(\frac{p\eta {|\nu|}\right), \quad {\rm as \quad p\eta \to 0.}}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-095: It should be stressed at the same time that retarded and advanced propagators do not receive growing with time contributions in the limit $\sqrt{\eta_1\eta_2 \to 0$, $\eta_1/\eta_2 = const$ \cite{Akhmedov:2013vka .}}
elements.sent-2411-096: Thus, only the Keldysh propagator contains the secular contribution, which, hence, cannot be e.g. absorbed into the self-energy and/or mode frequency shift.
elements.section-2411-003: Multi-loop contributions to the two-point functions
elements.sent-2411-097: At late times, when $\lambda^2\log(p\eta) \sim 1$, the perturbation expansion breaks down and we need to sum up at least the leading secular corrections from all loops.
elements.sent-2411-098: In order to write the system of appropriate Dyson-Schwinger equations, which does the summation of the leading corrections, we have to estimate which type of diagrams should be taken into account.
elements.sent-2411-099: Obviously, there are ladder (bead or chaplet type) diagrams which will give powers of the form $\left[\lambda^2\log(p\eta)\right]^n$, $n\geq 2$.
elements.sent-2411-100: In this section we show that the ``bubble inside bubble'' diagrams, such as those shown on Fig.\ref{Fig:BubbleInsideBubble , contribute subleading corrections.}
elements.sent-2411-101: In view of the fact that one-loop corrections to the retarded and advanced propagators do not contain any secular growth, one simple way to see this fact is to substitute the leading one loop correction (\ref{eq:correction_1-loop_EPP ) into the internal Keldysh lines of Fig.\ref{Fig:BubbleDiagram .}}
elements.sent-2411-103: This very important remark was not taken into account in \cite{Akhmedov:2013vka, Akhmedov:2017ooy .}
elements.sent-2411-104: Then it was corrected in \cite{Akhmedov:2019cfd , but without the revision of the Dyson-Schwinger equation and its solutions.}
elements.sent-2411-105: In this work we fill in this loophole in the research.
elements.sent-2411-106: The point is that if the diagrams shown on the Fig.\ref{Fig:BubbleInsideBubble are included in the leading approximation, then the resulting integral equation on the corrected Keldysh propagator is not linear and has redundant singular solutions \cite{Akhmedov:2017ooy .}}
elements.sent-2411-107: Nevertheless, in \cite{Akhmedov:2013vka it was shown that non-linear equations resumming diagrams appear in CPP and global dS.}
elements.sent-2411-108: That was shown for the case of principal series, and we will demonstrate the same for the light fields from the complementary series below.
elements.sent-2411-109: The system of Dyson-Schwinger equations is imposed on propagators and multipoint correlation functions.
elements.sent-2411-110: Hence, to single out the leading corrections we should check for potentially dangerous (growing with time) loop contributions to higher-point correlators as well.
elements.sent-2411-111: At the one loop level the correction to the three-point correlation function consists of diagrams shown on Fig.\ref{Fig:Vertex_1-loop .}
elements.figure-2411-003: \def\svgwidth{\textwidth} \includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{figures/Vertex_1-loop.pdf} \caption{One loop correction to the three-point function to be compared with the tree-level value in the limit $p_{1,2,3}\eta_{1,2,3}\rightarrow 0$. The subscripts of the fields in the correlators symbolically mean the corresponding time coordinate and the diagrams are written in the momentum representation.} \label{Fig:Vertex_1-loop}
elements.sent-2411-112: The loop corrections from the Fig.\ref{Fig:Vertex_1-loop have the following form modulo the overall coefficient: \beq \begin{aligned \Delta_{1-\text{loop }\left\langle\phi_1\phi_2\phi_3\right\rangle \sim \sim \delta^{(D-1)}\left(\bm{p _1+\bm{p _2+\bm{p _3\right)\cdot\lambda^{3}\cdot\int_{\eta_1 ^{\infty}\frac{d\eta_4 {\eta_4^{D }\int_{\eta_2 ^{\infty}\frac{d\eta_5 {\eta_5^{D }\int_{\eta_3 ^{\infty}\frac{d\eta_6 {\eta_6^{D }\rho\big(\bm{p _1\big|\eta_1,\eta_4\big)\rho\big(\bm{p _2\big|\eta_5,\eta_2\big)\rho\big(\bm{p _3\big|\eta_6,\eta_3\big)\times \times \int\frac{d^{D-1}\bm{k {(2\pi)^{D-1 G^K_{0}\big(\bm{k -\bm{p _2\big|\eta_4,\eta_5\big)G^K_{0}\big(\bm{k}\big|\eta_5,\eta_6\big)G^K_{0}\big(\bm{k +\bm{p _3\big|\eta_6,\eta_4\big).}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}

elements.sent-2411-117: It happens because in the multi-point correlation functions the ``volume'' factors $\eta^{\frac{D-1 {2 }$ in the modes (\ref{eq:field_decomposition ) and propagators (\ref{eq:propagators ) suppress the peculiar IR behavior of the light fields (\ref{eq:asymptotics ) when integrated over the internal conformal times of the diagrams.}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-118: Thus, we do not need to write the Dyson-Schwinger equations for the multi-point correlation functions when we sum up the leading secularly growing corrections of the form (\ref{eq:dominant_contr_1-loop_EPP ), and can keep these correlation functions of the tree-level form in the equations for the propagators.}
elements.sent-2411-119: However, let us stress that there is a strong IR correction at the one-loop order for the vertex function, which is the amputated diagram of the form shown on Fig.\ref{Fig:Vertex_1-loop , i.e. with removed external propagators in the expression (\ref{eq:vertex_1-loop-general_formula ).}}
elements.sent-2411-120: In fact, in this case the corresponding estimates give: \beq \begin{aligned \Delta_{1-\text{loop }\left\langle\phi_1\phi_2\phi_3\right\rangle^{\text{Amp.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-121: In \cite{Akhmedov:2017ooy it is pointed out that such a behavior of the vertex functions is connected to the appearance of the discrete set of states from the complementary series in the K\"all\'en-Lehmann decomposition for small enough masses of the scalar field.}
elements.sent-2411-122: Nevertheless, as we discussed above, these observations do not complicate the procedure of the resummation of the leading contributions of the form of powers of (\ref{eq:correction_1-loop_EPP ).}
elements.sent-2411-123: The observations that have been made in the previous sections allow us to state that at the leading approximation in the EPP in the limit $p\sqrt{\eta_1\eta_2 \rightarrow 0$, $\eta_1/\eta_2 = const$ we should sum up the bead or chaplet type diagrams constructed from the one depicted on Fig.\ref{Fig:BubbleDiagram .}}
elements.sent-2411-124: Such a summation has to be done only for the Keldysh propagator, while retarded and andavced propagators, and vertexes can be taken of the tree-level form.
elements.sent-2411-125: In order to solve this equation, we will use the following ansatz for the ``exact'' Keldysh propagator: \beq G^K(\bm{p |\eta_1,\eta_2) = A_{- ^2 \eta^{D-1}\frac{N\left(p\eta\right) {(p\eta)^{2\nu , \; \eta = \sqrt{\eta_1\eta_2 , \eeq which solves this equation under certain conditions in the limit that we consider, as we will see now.}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-126: In general, we are interested in solving the equation (\ref{eq:DS-equation_Complementary_EPP ) for different initial conditions for the Keldysh propagator, which carries the information about the quantum state of the scalar field at the exit surface\footnote{One should keep in mind that loop corrections respect the dS isometry in the EPP for the BD state \cite{polyakov2012infraredinstabilitysitterspace , \cite{Higuchi_2011 , \cite{hollands2010correlatorsfeynmandiagramsquantum , \cite{Akhmedov:2022uug .}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-127: But it is more interesting to trace the destiny of perturbations (perhaps spatially homogeneous) of the BD state that violate the dS isometry at the initial moment.. $p\eta \sim |\nu|$.
elements.sent-2411-128: In this paper we use the simplest choice.
elements.sent-2411-129: Namely, we assume some initial value for $N(p\eta)$ in (\ref{eq:ansatz_for_GK_1 ), $N_0 \equiv N(P_0) = 1+2n(P_0)-2\text{Re}\left\{\varkappa(P_0)\right\}$, which is imposed for some initial physical momentum $P_0 = (p\eta)_0$.}
elements.sent-2411-130: Such a choice preserves at least the following part of the dS isometry\footnote{It must be stressed here that this is a rather specific choice of the initial conditions from the physical perspective (see e.g. \cite{Akhmedov:2024ice for the related discussion).}}
elements.sent-2411-131: There are many more different ways to set up initial conditions, and they normally break the dS isometry.
elements.sent-2411-132: This point is discussed in a bit more details in the concluding section of this paper.
elements.sent-2411-133: Therefore, the resulting equation for the evolution of the quantum state takes the form \beq N(p\eta) \simeq N_0 + 4 \, A_{- \, \text{Im}\{A_{+}\}\, N_{0}\,\lambda^2\int_{1 ^{\frac{1 {\sqrt{p\eta }\frac{dv {v^{2\nu+1 }\int_{v ^{\frac{1 {p\eta v }\frac{du {u}\Big[N(p\eta\sqrt{uv ) + N_{0}\Big]\text{Im}\big\{F(v)\big\} + \mathcal{O (\lambda^2), \eeq where $A_\pm$ are defined in (\ref{eq:asymptotics ) and $F(v)$ is defined in (\ref{defFv ).}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-134: Let us stress again that the equation is valid in the approximations discussed in Section \ref{subSec:One_loop_Complementary_EPP .}
elements.sent-2411-135: The equation (\ref{eq:DS_for_N_Complementary_EPP ) in principle may have different solutions for different initial conditions.}
elements.sent-2411-136: An obvious ansatz for the solution can be taken of the form $N(p\eta) = C(p\eta)^{\alpha}$.
elements.sent-2411-137: If $\alpha > 0$, then $N(p\eta\sqrt{uv )\ll N_0$ in Eq. (\ref{eq:DS_for_N_Complementary_EPP ), as $p\eta \to 0$, and, hence, the ansatz is not appropriate, because it does not solve the equation under consideration.}}
elements.sent-2411-138: In contrast, if we assume that $\alpha < 0$, then $N(p\eta\sqrt{uv )\gg N_0$ and we obtain from (\ref{eq:DS_for_N_Complementary_EPP ) the equation defining $\alpha$: \beq \alpha \simeq -4\lambda^2 N_0 A_{-}\text{Im}\left\{A_{+}\right\}\int_{1 ^{\infty}\frac{dv {v^{2\nu+1 }\text{Im}\left\{F(v)\right\}v^{\frac{\alpha {2 , \eeq where we set the upper limit of the integral over $v$ to infinity due to its rapid convergence on much smaller interval.}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-139: In the leading order the solution for $\alpha$ is very close to zero: \beq \alpha \simeq -4\lambda^2 N_0 A_{-}\text{Im}\left\{A_{+}\right\}\int_{1 ^{\infty}\frac{dv {v^{2\nu+1 }\text{Im}\left\{F(v)\right\} + \mathcal{O (\lambda^4), \eeq providing that the initial value $N_0$, which can have both signs, should be such that $\alpha<0$.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-140: In this case the exact Keldysh propagator blows up in comparison with its tree-level value.
elements.sent-2411-142: Nevertheless this power-law growth of the Keldysh propagator doesn't overcome the volume factor, $\eta^{D-1}$, in (\ref{eq:field_decomposition ), (\ref{eq:propagators ) or (\ref{eq:ansatz_for_GK_1 ) and generally can be considered as an IR renormalization of the mass, making the situation somewhat similar to the one considered in \cite{Gautier:2013aoa,Guilleux:2016oqv,Serreau:2013psa in large-$N$ models.}}}}
elements.section-2411-006: A comment on the situation in the contracting Poincare patch
elements.sent-2411-143: In order to illustrate the situation where the dS isometry is inevitably broken at the loop order (even if at the tree-level it was respected) we examine here similar calculations in CPP for the complementary series.
elements.sent-2411-144: In CPP we consider the same initial Bunch-Davies state and the corresponding modes (\ref{eq:harmonics ) as in EPP, i.e. we also consider spatially homogeneous state, which of course in CPP, unlike the case of EPP, is unstable with respect to spatially inhomogeneous perturbations.}
elements.sent-2411-145: However, the effects that we consider below have nothing to do with this instability and are present e.g. in the upper part of the global dS space-time.
elements.sent-2411-146: The metric in CPP being expressed in conformal time is the same as in EPP, but the key difference is now the relation between conformal and cosmological time, $\eta=e^{t}$, and the arguments of theta-functions in (\ref{eq:propagators_list ) must be swapped between 1 and 2.}
elements.sent-2411-147: Hence, the conformal time, $\eta$, is ranging from $\eta=0$ at past infinity, $t=-\infty$, to $\eta = +\infty$ in the future infinity, $t = +\infty$.
elements.sent-2411-148: This choice of the mode functions and the state in CPP corresponds to the propagators that respect the proper Hadamard behavior and the dS isometry at the tree level.
elements.sent-2411-149: But this is not the only option for the initial state.
elements.sent-2411-150: In fact, it may be appropriate to consider in-modes in CPP, which are proportional to the Bessel function $h_{\nu}\sim Y_{\nu (p\eta)$ (see e.g. \cite{Akhmedov:2013xka, Akhmedov:2013vka ) rather than Hankel ones.}}
elements.sent-2411-151: This Bessel function is some linear combination of the Hankel functions $H_{\nu ^{(1)}$ and $H_{\nu ^{(2)}$.}}
elements.sent-2411-152: Furthermore, one should keep in mind that for other so called alpha-states, different from the BD one, which respect the dS isometry at tree-level, this symmetry is broken in the loops \cite{Akhmedov:2022uug even in EPP.}
elements.sent-2411-153: In a certain sense the latter fact is related to the non-Hadamard behavior of the corresponding propagators.
elements.sent-2411-154: In the case of CPP we encounter the secular divergence \cite{Krotov:2010ma , \cite{Akhmedov:2019cfd rather than the secular growth.}}
elements.sent-2411-155: In fact, one can straightforwardly see from (\ref{eq:1-loop_correction_general_formula_EPP ) that in EPP the $\eta_{3,4}$ integrals are bounded from below by the external time $\eta$ due to the causality of the Schwinger-Keldysh technique.}
elements.sent-2411-156: Then the resulting expression for the correction in EPP (\ref{eq:correction_1-loop_EPP ) is singular in the limit $\eta \rightarrow 0$.}
elements.sent-2411-157: Now, in the CPP the external conformal time, $\eta = \sqrt{\eta_1\eta_2}$, is the upper limit of the $\eta_{3,4}$ integrals rather than the lower one.
elements.sent-2411-158: Then the analogs of integrals in (\ref{eq:1-loop_correction_general_formula_EPP ) for CPP are divergent, because the lower bound in them is zero.}
elements.sent-2411-159: To cut the divergence we must introduce the lower bound of $\eta_{3,4}$ integration by imposing some initial conformal time $\eta_0$.
elements.sent-2411-160: This is the position of an initial Cauchy surface that cannot be taken it to zero (or $t_0 = \log \eta_0$ to past infinity) because of the singularity.
elements.sent-2411-161: This introduction of the initial Cauchy surface breaks the dS isometry at one loop level.
elements.sent-2411-162: Then the reasonable question arises whether this symmetry is restored after the resummation of the loop corrections or not, i.e. can one remove the cut $\eta_0$ from the loop-corrected answer or not? In the loop calculations in CPP for the light fields there are two situations that should be considered separately.
elements.sent-2411-163: First situation is appears for the modes with momentum $p \sqrt{\eta_1\eta_2 = p\eta > |\nu|$.}
elements.sent-2411-164: Then in the external legs we cannot use for the Hankel functions their approximate form for small arguments (\ref{eq:asymptotics ).}
elements.sent-2411-165: Hence, one can distinguish between the quantum level populations $n_{p}$ and the anomalous quantum average $\varkappa_{p}$ inside the whole correction to the Keldysh propagator as in (\ref{eq:Keldysh_at_large_times ).}
elements.sent-2411-166: To estimate the leading corrections to $n_{p}$ and $\varkappa_{p}$, as $p\eta_0 \to 0$, one can use for the Hankel functions inside $\eta_{3,4}$ integrals their asymptotic form for small arguments (\ref{eq:asymptotics ) when $\eta_{3,4}\leq\frac{|\nu| {p}$.}}
elements.sent-2411-167:
Then the gained contributions to these quantities in the one-loop order are as follows:  eq  egin{aligned n_p(\eta) \simeq  rac{\lambda^2 {(p\eta_0)^{2
u }\cdot rac{A_{- ^2 {

u + A_{+ ^{*} rac{1 {v^{2
u F(v)ight\} +\mathcal{O (\lambda^2), \end{aligned \eeq  eq  egin{aligned \varkappa_p(\eta) \simeq  rac{\lambda^2 {(p\eta_0)^{2

elements.sent-2411-168: Such contributions to $n_{p}$ and $\varkappa_{p}$ also appear in EPP, but in that case they are exactly cancel each other in the correction to the Keldysh propagator (\ref{eq:Keldysh_at_large_times ).}
elements.sent-2411-169: We see that in CPP for the modes with $p\eta > |\nu|$ this cancellation does not happen.
elements.sent-2411-170: The second case that should be considered in CPP is for the modes with the momentum $p\eta < |\nu|$.
elements.sent-2411-171: In this case one cannot separate the corrections to $n_p$ and $\varkappa_p$ as in (\ref{eq:Keldysh_at_large_times ) and should consider the correction to the whole Keldysh propagator as in (\ref{eq:Keldysh_large_times_complementary_EPP ).}}
elements.sent-2411-172: Then the one-loop correction is expressed as \beq \Delta_{1-\text{loop G^{K (\bm{p |\eta,\eta) = -\frac{A_{- ^2 {(p\eta)^{2\nu }\eta^{D-1}\cdot\lambda^2\log\left(\frac{\eta {\eta_0}\right)\cdot 8A_{-}\text{Im}\left\{A_{+}\right\}\int_{1 ^{\infty}\frac{dv {v v^{2\nu}\text{Im}\left\{F(v)\right\} + \mathcal{O}\left(\lambda^2\right), \eeq and the leading non-logarithmic corrections cancel each other, as it was the case in EPP.}}}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-173: Let us summarize and compare our observations for both in CPP and in EPP.
elements.sent-2411-174: In the case of EPP the integrals in (\ref{eq:1-loop_correction_general_formula_EPP ) are convergent, but have large contributions for such external momenta, $p$, that $p\eta \ll |\nu|$.}
elements.sent-2411-175: For other momenta the corrections are just suppressed by the coupling constant, $\lambda^2$.
elements.sent-2411-176: At the same time, in CPP independently of the value of momentum $p$ such integrals as in (\ref{eq:1-loop_correction_general_formula_EPP ) are divergent at the lower bound of integration over internal times $\eta_{3,4}$, because the upper and lower limits of these integrals are exchanged in CPP, as compared to the case of EPP.}
elements.sent-2411-177: One has to introduce the cutoff, $\eta_0$, at the lower bound of integration independently of the value of $p$.
elements.sent-2411-178: This is the position of the initial Cauchy surface.
elements.sent-2411-179: Furthermore, for such $p$ that $p\eta_0 < |\nu|$ there are large corrections to the Keldysh propagator.
elements.sent-2411-180: At the same time, for such $p$ that $p\eta_0 > |\nu|$ there are no large corrections.
elements.sent-2411-181: What is peculiar now, for such $p$ that $p\eta_0 < |\nu|$, but $p\eta > |\nu|$ ($\eta > \eta_0$) we have large power like (in $p\eta_0$) rather than logarithmic corrections, which are separately present in $n_p(\eta)$ and $\varkappa_p(\eta)$.
elements.sent-2411-182: At the same time for such $p$ that $p\eta < |\nu|$ we have only large logarithmic corrections, while power like corrections cancel each other in the whole expression for the corrected Keldysh propagator.
elements.sent-2411-183: Furthermore, it is straightforward to see that for the case\footnote{The case $p\eta > |\nu|$ should be considered separately.}
elements.sent-2411-184: Therefore, in contrast with respect to the case of EPP, we must take into account the ``loops inside loops'' in the leading order in powers of the parameter $\lambda^2 \log\left(\frac{\eta {\eta_0}\right)$.}
elements.sent-2411-185: Hence, in the corresponding Dyson-Schwinger equation, which sums leading loop corrections, we should use the exact Keldysh propagator also inside internal lines.
elements.sent-2411-186: This will lead to a non-linear integral equation for the exact Keldysh propagator in CPP.
elements.sent-2411-187: As shown for principal series in \cite{Akhmedov:2013vka, Akhmedov:2012dn the non-linear integral equations can lead to both singular solutions with the ``explosive'' behavior of the stress-energy tensor for a finite proper time and smooth solutions, depending on the initial conditions.}
elements.sent-2411-188: The complication with the complementary series is inapplicability of the kinetic approximation, which works when the function $N(p\eta)$ (or $n_p(\eta)$ and $\varkappa_p(\eta)$) can be treated as slow function in comparison with the modes.
elements.sent-2411-189: We will return to the situation in CPP and in global dS for the fields from the complementary series in future work.
elements.sent-2411-190: In this paper we revised the secular effects in dS space-time, focusing on the complementary series.
elements.sent-2411-191: We derived the Dyson-Schwinger equation, which sums up the leading secular contributions in the case of light fields in EPP.
elements.sent-2411-192: We corrected the observations of \cite{Akhmedov:2017ooy according to the observations that has been made in \cite{Akhmedov:2019cfd .}}
elements.sent-2411-193: Namely, we excluded the diagrams of the type shown in Fig.\ref{Fig:BubbleInsideBubble , as these diagrams are actually suppressed as compared to the bead or chaplet diagrams, and provide subleading contributions.}
elements.sent-2411-194: Additionally, we have showed that the higher-point correlation functions should not be considered in the resulting system of Dyson-Schwinger equations, even for sufficiently small masses $\nu>\frac{D-1 {6}$, and can be taken to have its tree-level form.}
elements.sent-2411-195: We then found the solution to this Dyson-Schwinger equation in one particular situation, when the initial values of quantum level populations and anomalous quantum averages are set at the initial physical momentum $P_0 = (p\eta)_{0}$.
elements.sent-2411-196: First, with this choice we preserve at least a part of the dS isometry, consisting of the rescalings $p\rightarrow \frac{1 {\alpha p, \;\eta\rightarrow\alpha\eta $ (see also \cite{Starobinsky:1994bd, Tsamis:2005hd for the related discussion).}}}
elements.sent-2411-197: Second, it allows for a straightforward solution of the Dyson-Schwinger equation with a clear physical meaning.
elements.sent-2411-198: It is interesting to explore different forms of initial conditions in EPP.
elements.sent-2411-199: One option is to fix some initial time $\eta_0$, which can be considered as the time after which the self-interaction of the fields was adiabatically turned on, then define the initial values of $n_p(\eta_0)$ and may be even $\varkappa_p(\eta_0)$.
elements.sent-2411-200: However, taking $\eta_0$ to past infinity in this case is not possible, as it would lead to a divergent physical energy density.
elements.sent-2411-202: These options for the initial conditions violate the dS isometry at the tree-level.
elements.sent-2411-203: But their consideration still may provide further insights into the solutions of the Dyson-Schwinger equations for large IR contributions, depending on the initial state of the system at the beginning of its evolution.
elements.sent-2411-204: In addition, we have considered loop corrections in CPP in Section \ref{Sec:Complementary_CPP .}
elements.sent-2411-205: While in the case of EPP the integrals in (\ref{eq:1-loop_correction_general_formula_EPP ) are convergent, but have large contributions for such external momenta, $p$, that $p\eta \ll |\nu|$, in CPP we encounter quite a different situation.}
elements.sent-2411-206: In the latter case independently of the value of momentum $p$ such integrals as in (\ref{eq:1-loop_correction_general_formula_EPP ) are divergent at the lower bound of integration over internal times $\eta_{3,4}$, because the upper and lower limits of these integrals are exchanged in CPP, as compared to the case of EPP.}
elements.sent-2411-207: At the same time for such $p$ that $p\eta < |\nu|$ we have only large logarithmic corrections, while power like corrections cancel each other in the complete expression for the corrected Keldysh propagator.
elements.sent-2411-208: We will derive the Dyson-Schwinger equation, which sums these corrections up, in our future work and also consider the situation in global dS.
elements.sent-2411-209: Finally, in Appendix \ref{App:Principal_EPP we demonstrated that for the principal series, unlike the complementary series, there is a static solution for the exact $n_{p (\eta)$ and $\varkappa_{p (\eta)$ when the scalar field has sufficiently large mass.}}}

elements.section-2411-008: A brief comment on the principal series in the expanding Poincare patch
elements.sent-2411-213: Secular effects in EPP for the principal series were investigated in \cite{Krotov:2010ma, Akhmedov:2013vka .}
elements.sent-2411-214: In this appendix we just reconsider the derivation of the Dyson-Schwinger equation, making sure that the ``bubble inside bubble'' diagrams from Fig.\ref{Fig:BubbleInsideBubble are not included as the corrections of subleading order.}
elements.sent-2411-216: Please note here the presence of the extra suppressing factor $e^{-\pi \nu}$, which is present on top of the coupling constant $\lambda^2$.
elements.sent-2411-217: Furthermore, every higher loop brings extra power of this factor.
elements.sent-2411-218: Then for large masses there is perhaps no even necessity to sum up the loop corrections, because it demands too many e-foldings to make the loop corrections comparable to the tree-level contributions to the correlation functions.
elements.sent-2411-219: Nevertheless let us consider the Dyson-Schwinger equation, which resums the leading corrections, because for sufficiently small $\nu$ the factor $e^{-\pi \nu}$ is essentially absent.
elements.sent-2411-220: Then in the kinetic approximation we can write the Dyson-Schwinger equation for the leading logarithms (\ref{eq:n_and_kappa_principal_series ) \cite{Akhmedov:2013vka : \beq \begin{aligned G^{K (\bm{p |\eta,\eta) = G^{K _{0 (\bm{p |\eta,\eta)+ \lambda^2\int\frac{d^{D-1}\bm{k {(2\pi)^{D-1 }\frac{d\eta_{3 {\eta_{3 ^{D }\frac{d\eta_{4 {\eta_{4 ^{D \bigg[-\frac{1 {2}\theta(\eta_3-\eta)\theta(\eta_4-\eta)\times \times \rho\big(\bm{p}\big|\eta,\eta_3\big)\left(G^{K^2 _{0}\big(\bm{k -\bm{p}\big|\eta_3,\eta_4\big) - \frac{1 {4}\rho^2\big(\bm{k -\bm{p}\big|\eta_3,\eta_4\big)\right)\rho\big(\bm{p}\big|\eta_4,\eta\big) + + \theta(\eta_4-\eta_3)\theta(\eta_3-\eta)\rho\big(\bm{p}\big|\eta,\eta_3\big)\rho\big(\bm{k -\bm{p}\big|\eta_3,\eta_4\big) G^K_{0}\big(\bm{k -\bm{p}\big|\eta_3,\eta_4\big)G^K\big(\bm{p}\big|\eta_4,\eta\big) + +\theta(\eta_3-\eta_4)\theta(\eta_4-\eta)G^K\big(\bm{p}\big|\eta,\eta_3\big)\rho\big(\bm{k -\bm{p}\big|\eta_3,\eta_4\big) G^K_{0}\big(\bm{k -\bm{p}\big|\eta_3,\eta_4\big)\rho\big(\bm{p}\big|\eta_4,\eta\big) \bigg] .}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}

elements.sent-2411-222: Therefore, in the case of sufficiently large masses of a scalar field with Bunch-Davies vacuum as the initial state, the system only slightly restructures the initial state and passes into some energy density on top of the out-vacuum as a result of a long evolution in the expanding background.. \bibliographystyle{unsrt.}
